title: Assault
content_markdown:
#### Management

- Be aware of risks to yourself and others 
- Consider police assistance
- Ensure scene control and safety.

- As much as is practical, be wary of evidence preservation at the scene 
- Wear gloves, watch where you walk, etc.
- Treat injuries as per appropriate CMG- Attention to detail (such as full name, location, times, others present) is very important. - Be sure to document, in detail, any injuries/markings found during examination.
- Report any suspicion, belief or concern about child abuse or neglect as soon as possible.

#### Specifically for Sexual Assault

- Be aware that some patients may resent physical contact from carers.
- Avoid judgemental comments 
- Do not allow personal beliefs to interfere with any communication.

- Ensure that every assessment or treatment procedure is explained fully and consent is gained before it is conducted. 
- Exceptions to this include patients with a decreased LOC, or with life threatening injuries. 
- Articles of evidence, such as clothing, must be taken with the patient (use a paper bag).
- Discourage the patient from showering or washing.
- Consider offering to bring a support person with the patient, if appropriate. - Do not give details of the assault over the radio.\u2003
